_doc: Audit-5 B5 deterministic-replay corpus. The audit-3 ledger preserves only the post-extractJson judge OBJECT, not the raw failing judge TEXT, so the literal 22 B5 strings are unrecoverable. This git-tracked fixture reproduces the failure SHAPES instead (the brief explicitly permits 'or a captured fixture'). Each sample is piped through extractJson() then validateJudgeShape(); expect_ok is the required {ok}. P1 target: exactly 6 nonconforming (ok:false) + 2 conforming (ok:true).

samples.name: fenced_valid_json
samples.note: extractJson already strips ```json fences (do not re-handle)

samples.name: truncated_json
samples.note: maxTokens cut mid-token — brace-walk never closes -> extractJson null

samples.name: prose_then_truncated
samples.note: model wrote reasoning prose first, JSON cut before close
samples.raw: The app's claimed answer is medically correct per board-level evidence. {"app_answer_correct":

samples.name: prose_only_no_json
samples.raw: Yes — the app's claimed answer is correct. This aligns with Hazzard 8e and GRS8 guidance on the management of frailty in the very old.

samples.name: fenced_then_truncated
samples.note: leading fence stripped, unterminated string inside -> extractJson null

samples.name: json_string_bool
samples.note: parses fine BUT app_answer_correct is the STRING "true", not a boolean

samples.name: json_missing_aac
